text: If you book forty rooms, I will hold the rate.
If you pay the deposit today, breakfast will be complimentary.
If we travel in the low season, the price will drop.
Hi Andrea, thanks for the proposal. Could you quote me a group rate for forty guests in October?
Of course, Miguel. If you confirm forty guests, I will give you our best net rate.
That sounds fair. And what happens if we need to cancel some rooms?
If you cancel thirty days before arrival, there will be no surcharge at all.
Good to know. October is peak season, so I expected a higher price.
If you book before the end of the month, I will hold the low-season rate for you.
Excellent. And is breakfast included in the package?
Yes, breakfast is complimentary. If you sign today, I will add a welcome dinner for your group.
Could you quote me a group rate?
If you book early, I will offer a discount.
A small surcharge applies in peak season.

Hi Andrea, this is Miguel from the Grand Coast Hotel. Thank you for your enquiry about our group rates for next season. Here is a quick summary. If you book more than thirty rooms, we will offer you our best net rate, with a ten percent markup instead of the usual fifteen. During peak season, a small surcharge applies at the weekend, but if you travel in the low season, that surcharge disappears completely. We ask for a twenty percent deposit to confirm any group, and our cancellation policy is flexible: if you cancel thirty days in advance, you pay nothing. Breakfast is always complimentary. Call me back and we will finalise the package.

Welcome to this short segment on smart negotiating. Here is one simple rule that every good salesperson knows: never give a discount for nothing. Always ask for something in return. In English, we do this with the first conditional. You say, if you book early, I will offer a better rate, or if you commit to thirty rooms, we will waive the surcharge. Notice the pattern: if plus the present simple for the condition, and will plus the verb for the result. This little structure protects your prices and makes the client feel they have earned the deal. So next time you talk numbers, remember: state your condition first, then your offer.
If you book early, I will offer a discount.
If you pay the deposit, breakfast will be complimentary.
Could you quote me a group rate?
A small surcharge applies in peak season.
